candidates.observable_statement: GE Vernova will spend nearly $600 million on U.S. factories and facilities over two years.
candidates.capital_flow_implication: Capital flow of approximately $600 million into U.S. manufacturing facilities, creating over 1,500 jobs.
candidates.physical_implication: Construction of new factories and facilities, expansion of existing plants, procurement of equipment, and hiring of workforce.

candidates.observable_statement: The investment will create over 1,500 jobs and focus on gas power, grid, nuclear and onshore wind manufacturing sites.
candidates.capital_flow_implication: Capital flow into the U.S. energy manufacturing sectors—gas power, grid, nuclear, and onshore wind—supporting capacity expansion.
candidates.physical_implication: Expansion of manufacturing capacity for energy equipment, procurement of specialized machinery, and workforce hiring.

candidates.observable_statement: Rockwell Automation plans to build a new factory in Wisconsin as part of a $2 billion effort to strengthen its U.S. operations.
candidates.capital_flow_implication: Capital is flowing into Wisconsin's manufacturing sector through a $2B investment in a new factory.
candidates.physical_implication: Construction of a new 1+ million square foot manufacturing and warehouse campus.

candidates.observable_statement: Rowan Digital Infrastructure has broken ground on a 300MW hyperscale campus outside San Antonio. The $900 million project is tailored to a top five U.S. technology company, with an initial 60MW expected in early 2026.
candidates.capital_flow_implication: Capital inflow of $900 million into the data center infrastructure system in the San Antonio region, expanding capacity by 300MW.
candidates.physical_implication: Construction of a 300MW data center campus, including building facilities, power and cooling infrastructure, and network connectivity.

candidates.observable_statement: The $900 million project is tailored to a top five U.S. technology company, with an initial 60MW expected in early 2026.
candidates.capital_flow_implication: Capital inflow to support an initial 60MW capacity deployment in early 2026, indicating a phased expansion of data center capacity.
candidates.physical_implication: Installation of 60MW of data center capacity, including servers, power, cooling, and networking equipment.

candidates.observable_statement: EdgeCore is expanding its Mesa campus by adding nearly 44 acres
candidates.capital_flow_implication: Capital is flowing into land acquisition and development for the Mesa data center campus.
candidates.physical_implication: Purchase or lease of 44 acres of land for future data center expansion.

candidates.observable_statement: lifting total capacity from more than 200MW to more than 450MW
candidates.capital_flow_implication: Capital is flowing into power infrastructure and equipment to increase data center capacity.
candidates.physical_implication: Installation of additional power supply, cooling, and infrastructure to support 450MW capacity.

candidates.observable_statement: to meet urgent hyperscale demand for AI and cloud-ready capacity
candidates.capital_flow_implication: Capital is flowing into procurement of servers, networking equipment, and related technology to support AI and cloud services.
candidates.physical_implication: Purchase and installation of servers, networking gear, and storage systems to support increased capacity.

candidates.observable_statement: EdgeCore began construction at a 114MW campus in Northern Virginia with delivery expected in 2026.
candidates.capital_flow_implication: Capital is flowing from EdgeCore into construction, procurement, and local labor markets to build a new data center campus.
candidates.physical_implication: Construction of a 114MW data center campus in Northern Virginia.

candidates.observable_statement: EdgeCore is also topping out additional facilities on its 450MW campus in Mesa, Arizona.
candidates.capital_flow_implication: Capital is flowing from EdgeCore into construction and procurement for expanding its 450MW campus in Mesa, Arizona.
candidates.physical_implication: Expansion of a 450MW data center campus in Mesa, Arizona.

candidates.observable_statement: Hitachi Energy will build a $457 million transformer production facility in South Boston, Virginia
candidates.capital_flow_implication: Capital flow of $457 million into the U.S. transformer manufacturing sector via construction of a new facility
candidates.physical_implication: Construction of a transformer production plant in South Boston, Virginia, including procurement of equipment, materials, and labor

candidates.observable_statement: Hitachi Energy will make more than $500 million in other investments to meet growing need for U.S. grid equipment
candidates.capital_flow_implication: Capital flow of $500+ million into the U.S. grid equipment manufacturing and related supply chain
candidates.physical_implication: Potential expansion of manufacturing capacity, procurement of equipment, and development of new facilities or upgrades

candidates.observable_statement: Jabil plans to spend $500 million to expand its U.S. manufacturing footprint in the Southeast to support surging data center demand.
candidates.capital_flow_implication: Capital inflow of $500M into North Carolina manufacturing sector for an AI data center facility.
candidates.physical_implication: Construction of a new AI data center facility and expansion of manufacturing footprint.

candidates.observable_statement: AVL Manufacturing will invest $56 million to establish its first U.S. production facility in North Carolina.
candidates.capital_flow_implication: Capital inflow into the U.S. manufacturing sector, specifically North Carolina, for the production of industrial enclosures used in large-scale power generators.
candidates.physical_implication: Construction of a new manufacturing plant, procurement of equipment, and creation of approximately 300 jobs.

candidates.observable_statement: Hitachi Energy announced a $250 million investment to expand global production of transformer components, with more than 40% dedicated to the U.S.
candidates.capital_flow_implication: Capital flow of $250M into transformer component manufacturing globally, with a significant portion directed to U.S. production.
candidates.physical_implication: Expansion of transformer component production facilities, likely new factories or plant expansions.

candidates.observable_statement: Hitachi Energy announced a $250 million investment to expand global production of transformer components, with more than 40% dedicated to the U.S.
candidates.capital_flow_implication: Capital flow of at least $100M (40% of $250M) into U.S. transformer component manufacturing capacity.
candidates.physical_implication: Construction or expansion of U.S. transformer component production facilities.

candidates.observable_statement: DTE signed a 1.4GW agreement to serve a hyperscale data center
candidates.capital_flow_implication: Capital will flow into DTE's generation and distribution infrastructure to supply the 1.4GW load for the hyperscale data center.
candidates.physical_implication: Construction or upgrade of generation units and transmission/distribution lines to deliver 1.4GW to the data center.

candidates.observable_statement: The utility now has a $30 billion investment pipeline for generation, distribution and other infrastructure from 2026 to 2030
candidates.capital_flow_implication: Capital will flow into DTE's generation, distribution, and other infrastructure projects over the next five years.
candidates.physical_implication: Construction of new generation facilities, upgrades to transmission lines, expansion of distribution infrastructure, and other related projects.

candidates.observable_statement: DTE sees another 7GW of potential large loads
candidates.capital_flow_implication: Capital may flow into DTE's infrastructure to accommodate an additional 7GW of large loads.
candidates.physical_implication: Potential construction of new generation units and upgrades to transmission/distribution lines to support the additional load.

candidates.observable_statement: Exelon utilities' pipeline of data centers and other high-density load projects more than doubled to 17GW.
candidates.capital_flow_implication: Exelon is allocating capital to develop new data center projects, indicating a flow of investment into the data center sector.
candidates.physical_implication: Construction of new data centers and associated infrastructure to support 17GW of additional load.

candidates.observable_statement: The company expects positive load growth and called for new approaches to ensure adequate power supplies.
candidates.capital_flow_implication: Exelon is planning to invest in transmission upgrades or new capacity to meet the increased load from data centers.
candidates.physical_implication: Upgrades to transmission lines, substations, or new interconnections to support 17GW of new load.
